channel.title: /biz/ Active (filtered, with replies)
channel.description: Filtered out: general, gme, bbbyq. OP + first 3 replies + last 30 replies.

item.title: BEARISH ON COSTCO — 109 replies — 345.1/hr
item.content:encoded:
BEARISH ON COSTCO

Open thread • Replies: 109 • Updated: 2026-07-08 06:45 UTC

OP

People are scamming costcos lenient return policy now. Should we short COST?

Video:
https://x.com/Basedgoymemes/status/2073860759269077199

Early replies (first 3)

62449396
based

62449400
I wouldn't call jeets 'people'

62449441
Fuck this racist, post the person who started to do this was some white bitch, she did it for 12 years

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62455754
>>62454135

62455759
>>62455686
You cant breed, kektop

62455766
>>62453370

62455771
>>62455759
Post stack
But you won't because you're poor and you'll always be poor.

62455822
>>62455771
Rocks? Lmao what a loser

62455829
>>62455822
Sour grapes

62455840
>>62455822
You can't even afford rocks? How poor are you? Answer: extremely poor.

62455845
>>62453347
Indian immigrants are the richest because they all want to escape their country. Most other countries are livable and civilized, so rich people from other countries like to stay in their own country.

Nothing to be proud of.

62455854
>>62455845
Your country is a shithole and you hate it

62455855
>>62455759
Indian men are at the absolute bottom of the sexual pecking order in Western nations.

Projection.

62455859
>>62455854
No country is a worse shithole than India Pakistan Bangladesh. Not sure what you meant by that

62455884
>>62449441
t. brown piece of garbage kek

62455902
>>62449769
You're an invading piece of shit.
I make a point of openly yelling at one brown retard every day at least; you fuckers were born to be yelled at.
Fucking retards who can't follow the most basic rules of civilization, third world trash

62455914
>>62455859
You white incel losers hate your own country and constantly wish it is destroyed. Totally bizarre behavior

62455930
>>62455902
Cry more incel, 1000 of my relatives invade every day. Its our country niw

62455936
>>62449441
Please leave anytime.

62455942
>>62455914
>incel losers
You're an expert on the subject from your daily first hand experience

62456019
>>62455942
I would like to thank the white incel losers for letting us have your jobs, your women and your country

62456057
>>62449441

>self reporting JEET gets caught online defending his street shitting brother

Imagine the smell of that chair

62456250
>>62456057
Kek

62456293
>>62455930
Kek, we stopped letting you dirty fuckers in last year, we have enough people serving coffee and doing menial jobs already.
You faggots are all 5'6" and stink like either vile BO or a whole bottle of nigger-scented baby powder wash.
Being brown and ugly is a curse from god himself; you are marked brown like shit and can never wash it off. Ugly jeet kek

62456323
>>62449769
Your skin colour is the same as my poop
>>62451853
>jelly
The only "jelly" thing is your jamboi covered body

62456610
>>62449441
Yes this saar is correct do not be insulting India sats, India is in the bag!!

62457469
IZZATED

62457562
>>62457469
Feels good to be a winner, conquering new lands, getting rich. America is so welcoming!

62457973
>>62457562
Cope

62458144
>>62454135
They've conquered my city, not my mind. They are rude, inconsiderable, foul-smelling pricks and are insufferable to be around. They practically camp out at CostCo now. I'd rather shop at smaller, more expensive stores just to avoid their presence. There is nothing I can do about it.

62458606
>>62458144
Canadian ?

62458608
>>62458144
There is plenty you could do but you value your comfort over your country

62458622
>>62449846
LMAO

item.title: I've been a NEET for a long time and I'm finally trying to get a job. — 108 replies — 220.7/hr
item.content:encoded:
I've been a NEET for a long time and I'm finally trying to get a job.

Open thread • Replies: 108 • Updated: 2026-07-08 06:35 UTC

OP

I've been a NEET for a long time and I'm finally trying to get a job. I'm not young, 40 next year, and don't have a job history or references. High school graduate.
How fucked am I?

Early replies (first 3)

62449979
I live in California, too.

62449982
What did you do for the last 23 years? Hard to help without more context. Do you have skills?

62449989
>>62449982
Depression and NEET means not in education employment or training.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62453557
>>62453510
Video editing is a shitload of work hours, if you reads OP shitpost you'll quickly understand it's out of his reach. He's basically asking if we can give him a winning lottery ticket, and when we tell him it doesn't work that way, he whines.

62453686
>>62453557
You would need a computer for that and an Internet connection. The desktop unused to have died several years ago and I'm only allowed to use the wifi with my phone.
I would have to move out completely first.

62453688
Also, if you guys could calm down on the personal attacks? I didn't do anything to deserve that shit. Whatever boggeyman lives in your head isn't me.

62453723
God, it feels like I'm having panic attack after panic attack.

62453813
>>62453723
honest to god you should go work on a boat/ship, get paid to roam the seas and hear stuff on the radio and sleep in the middle of the darkness of the ocean and occasionally see whales.

62453981
>>62449961
This is why I tell my neet friend to grt out and go grab a job, even packing groceries before you reach 40 something. YOU WILL REGRET NEETING

62454510
seconding personal home aide/caregiver
gotta get your shoe in the door somehow

https://www.uscareerinstitute.edu/online-caregiver-school
tuition: $485
2-4 months online part time training

62454560
Become a trans prostitute

62455526
I guess I need to see a dentist soon. That's next to do, I guess

62455565
Oh, is this the bump limit? Threads about to slide off the board?
>>62454560
You're insane or a glowie.

62455632
>>62454560
Can always start with trans feet pics. Get them all pedicured and female-passing first of course.

62455644
Are you artistically inclined?
No shit, you may be able to draw furry porn. Those guys still pay out the ass for commissions and even though AI is a thing a lot of furries refuse to use it to get their porn.

62455814
>>62449961
Good call making this on a Monday so you get the extra seethe from wagies that had to go back to work.

62455832
>>62455814
I hadn't thought of that. Lol.

62455843
>>62450003
You're practically dead already, I mean come on, can you really say you've lived at all as a NEET? And Hegseth in his infinite retardation raised the enlistment cut-off to 42 so it's an option.

62456054
>>62455832
You're not completely screwed, by the way. You'll have to work entry-level jobs for a bit to build references and show stability, though. In order of priority highest to lowest:
>phone/internet
You're posting here so I assume you have either one of these. Phone is important.
>dental
Try federally funded health centers, medicaid, dental schools, county clinics, and free clinics
>getting care for your mom
You can't even take care of yourself so try to find your mom some care. Try eldercare locator to start.
>transportation
If you're rural and don't have transportation that needs to change. The end goal here should be getting your license to drive, but if you never drove before that could take months so you'll need to find something in the meantime.
>job
If you're rural this might have to wait until you have transportation, but the goal is entry-level work to build a resume/show stability. If they ask about work history tell them you were out of the workforce for family caregiving and health reasons.

also apply for any assistance programs you can. have no shame in doing so. they're there for people in your situation

62456107
>>62449961
Your options are disability, construction or the military. Say you were caring for your mom. I'd try the military first while you are still eligible. If they declare you ineligible then either try trade school or use it as an excuse to claim disability.

62456122
I can't get an angle on my teeth to take photos, I'll have to get a dental mirror later.

62456853
>>62456122
Nope, sorry. My phone camera can't take pictures worth shit. At least nothing seems to be infected.

62457593
>>62450111

How is that a bad thing? Thats a good thing.

62457793
>>62451426
I hire and fire guys all the time. Honesty is important and if I catch a whiff of a liar in an interview its over, but in your case this anon is right. You have to lie on your resume, a 20 year gap would not make it past HR to me ever. You must research enough to be able to answer questions about what you put on there as work history so don't go too crazy. Such as explain to me in detail exactly what you did day to day at your previous made up job. Good luck.

62457849
I got into tech support without prior knowledge at 39. It's only replying to customer emails and phone calls.

Yes it doesn't pay much (40k Euros here in Germany) but it's enough for rent and an ok living. Not enough to have a car though.
The best part is that's it's depending on the company 30%-100% remote and honestly...it's easy work.
Yes A.I. will replace you in the next couple of years but there are maybe 2 years left to make a living with it.

62457884
>>62449961
You all speak about entry level jobs, starting his own business, or the (((military))).
But don't you anglo people have low level public servant exams? Class IV employee.
They are super easy, basically, to pass the exam, you need to know how to write, to count, and to have a minimum of general knowledge.
Once you pass the exam, they don't care even if you are 50. Sure the wage is minimum, and you have absolutely no way to evolve professionally. But they will not ask question. You only have to pass the exam.

62457969
>>62453510
Where did he find the clients?

62457986
>>62453537
Is the card market too inflated/saturated to bother anymore? Or do you still scalp?

62458076
>>62457793
>Honesty is important and if I catch a whiff of a liar in an interview its over, but in your case this anon is right. You have to lie on your resume, a 20 year gap would not make it past HR to me ever. You must research enough to be able to answer questions about what you put on there as work history so don't go too crazy. Such as explain to me in detail exactly what you did day to day at your previous made up job.

This is a low key admission job history doesnt matter.

62458190
>>62457884
No that doesn’t exist here. You can’t get a government job unless you’re a gay disabled Jewish black female veteran.

62458213
>>62449961
Do a trade or some type of apprenticeship through military or come to Australia and do FIFO (fly in fly out work). High pay, stack LINK. There's always hope fren

62458240
>>62450003
Depends on your country, but it's a good place to save by milking the free stuff.
Pick a civie equivalent job that isn't too physical and coast.

If you're not stupid enough to buy expensive cars and burn money on strippers you can save a lot (hookers are a more efficient use of spending money on women).
Get in, get out and collect gibs if you Jew your medical correctly

62458604
>>62449961
why do retards who ask how to get jobs never, EVER talk about their skills, experience (yes, you can be a NEET and still have experience even if you have never had a job), hobbies (that can be converted to jobs) and interests?
not asking about this particular OP, but about people in general...

item.title: Why do people act like buying a house is a good investment >Property taxes >Maintenance >Repairs because something WILL… — 54 replies — 184.4/hr
item.content:encoded:
Why do people act like buying a house is a good investment >Property taxes >Maintenance >Repairs because something WILL…

Open thread • Replies: 54 • Updated: 2026-07-08 06:46 UTC

OP

Why do people act like buying a house is a good investment
>Property taxes
>Maintenance
>Repairs because something WILL go wrong
>Even if you're "beating inflation" with a mortgage you're paying much more than its actual cost
>Closing costs
>All just so you can sell it (likely at a loss when considering inflation) so you can buy a slightly less expensive house
It's not that buying a house is a bad purchase if you can afford it, but acting like it's somehow an "investment" is absolutely retarded, you'd probably make more back from buying Pokemon cards

Early replies (first 3)

62450381
>>62450377
You're supposed to buy houses on a discount
>t. Selling for double what I purchased in 2023 after $20K in upgrades LOL

62450391
>>62450377
Idk I just want one to live in, but maybe they rent it that way person who rents pays bills and when he break something you can tell him to pay for it and then when they don't give a fuck anymore they sell it

62450397
>cbf moving every year because the landlord decides they a wankers and want to sell to some Chinese immigrants who took our jobs

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62456023
>>62456017
>Waaaaaaaaah im a loser cuz of old people not giving me their house

62456036
>>62452006
Last time the market crashed millions of homeowners went underwater.

62456053
>>62450446
It's very easy to lose money owning a house, if you buy a house in an area that then suffers from ghettofication

62456063
Rentoids will spend 30 years paying off someone else's house just to tell you you're bad at investing.
Bull owns the land.
Cuck pays the bull every month.
If you don't own land by retirement, you got outplayed.

62456064
yeah horrible investment
rent is due on the 1st wagie btw the price is going up 5% due to ongoing maintenance issues. please understand have a nice day

62456116
>>62450377
Housing is usually a good investment, because, in many countries, the line is artificially made to go up over time, such as by constraining supply. The housing market is deliberately designed as a giant wealth transfer scheme from younger generation to the older generation, because old people have high turnout on election day, and young people don't. In a well-functioning housing market, the value of a house would slowly fall over time, in line with physical depreciation of the house.

62456159
>>62450377
I agree. It's land that is the real asset. Bought with cash; you own nothing if you have a mortgage.

62456166
>>62456116
This. Property is more of a commodity. The price stays high because of artificial supply constraints.

62456177
>>62450381
bragging that he can't afford to move

62456181
>>62450446
yeah but I can rent for half the price of a mortgage and then the other half gets 10% returns tax free and also I get to move when I want to because I don't have a family

62456269
>>62450377
It's for really rich people who need diversified sources of investments but aren't so rich they can go to a hedge fund. Plus you can actually live there, and you can't live in a hedge fund.

62456282
>>62456036
being under water is only a problem if you want to sell or you can’t pay your mortgage.
you bought your house to live in it, right? you can still live in a house if you owe more than it’s worth.

62456296
>>62456282
The oppurtunity cost fucks you if your 600k house goes down to 450k, the average normie would rope.

62456329
>>62450377
All of that beats renting.

Because renting means you give someone else your money. And at the end of it you can't get any of it back.

62456336
get a couple houses and a couple rentoids then prepare to make that a job for the next 15 years

62456380
>>62456177
>t. rentnigger in bumfuckville who thinks everyone else on this forum is as poor as him
LOL have fun with your 10% returns on $300 every month Cletus. I'm selling for $300K and moving in to my parents. What should I do with that wad in addition to my vanguard account? :)

62456434
>>62456380
If you sold a house for 300k you are the Cletus in bumfuck nowhere.

62456628
>>62456434
>preferring to be Cornelius in bitchfuck somewhere over Cletus in bumfuck nowhere

62456713
>>62453548
And on top of all that, get this, you can live in it and keep your stuff there.

62457169
>>62456017
they actually split out the value of the land and the value of the house on your taxes. So no, clearly it's not the land going up in value if the land only accounts for a fraction of the total.

and if that's not proof enough, you can actually buy vacant land most places and compare the cost to buying a house and learn that houses go up in cost far faster than bare land.

interesting attempt at reason though. Your failure was in not trying to disprove your silly idea. You just thought it up and ran with it. Leaving it very easy to disprove for anyone that wants to.

62457177
>>62450377
all you really need to know are that homeowners are generally much wealthier than renters,
and
Homeowners can sell their house and go back to renting any time they want but almost never do. All your cope about why owning a home sucks doesn't seem to actually persuade anyone. People rent and then buy a home and never rent again because renting sucks and owning is much better.

62457179
>>62457177
these threads are just made by either a bot or some discord tranny. We can the same one every few days. Don't reply in good faith

62457226
>>62457177
the top 0.000000001% do not own residential properties.

62457260
>>62457179
It's like doing the daily crossword. I come on 4chan and look for retarded ideas. They're easy to find here, almost the entire site is retardation. Then I try to identify the exact problems with the reasoning behind the ideas, and find a succinct way to disprove it. I'll usually post this disproof because it drives 4chan nuts when people disagree with their stupidity. They spout more nonsense in defense of their earlier nonsense and I get to analyze and diagnose that shit as well. Perhaps respond to it if it's particularly stupid.

it's a hobby. Keeps my critical thinking skills sharp and reminds me that most humans are dumber than children.

62457295
>>62457260
but anon, they're being retarded on purpose. You're losing

62457303
>>62457295
their motives have nothing to do with my hobby
and from what I've seen almost nobody here is pretending to be retarded. They might think they're pretending, but they're not.

>You're losing
there's nothing to win or lose

62458442
>>62450446
>It’s hard to lose money from owning a house.
Nigga what? You lose opportunity cost of down payment, you lose maintenance, you lose insurance, you lose interest payments, you lose opportunity cost of moving for better jobs. Houses you live in are never an asset.

62458496
>>62450377
Because its good for
>Banks
>Financial advisors
>Accountants
>Lawyers
>Real Estate agents
>Construction industry
>Furniture industry
But if you stop and actually think about it for yourself, its fucking stupid. Rent and actual assets is way more liberating and wealth creating.

62458570
>>62450377
Mortgages are the most accessible form of leverage known to and accepted by people. Here at least you can have zero downpayment, borrow 500k and 'gamble' it into RE. If it pays off, that is fucking sweet.

62458626
>>62458496
Rentoid cope

item.title: Has Bitcoin become too dependent on ETFs and macro news? — 26 replies — 104.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Has Bitcoin become too dependent on ETFs and macro news?

Open thread • Replies: 26 • Updated: 2026-07-08 07:00 UTC

OP

Seems like every BTC pump or dump is blamed on ETF flows or macro headlines.

>Is that just the reality now, or are people overlooking other factors?

Early replies (first 3)

62450753
>>62450728
Its 100% cz dependent, just tradingview has to write something to the price action.

62450760
>>62450728
Btc does this thing where it lulls the newfag into a false sense of superiority. Both in bull and bear markets. In a bull market, it tricks newfags into thinking it will go up forever and traps them in overleveraged longs and in bear markets, it tricks them into it never going back up and they either sell low, sell covered calls or lever up short and get milked. Just hold spot and don't pay attention for a decade.

62450902
>>62450753
cz?

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62450909
>>62450760
In short = manipulation

62450916
>>62450902

62450937
>>62450916
Wait....what??

62450950
>thinks news drives the market

62450961
>>62450760
>just hold spot
>t. baggy
If you bought Bitcoin at the end of 2017 and held for 9 years, you would currently have no more value against gold than when you bought.

62450971
>>62450937
>not knowing who cz is

62451749
>>62450971
CZ is just another clown

62451999
>>62451749
A hardcore clown

62452810
>>62451999
the king of clown

62453044
>>62450961
I'm planning to buy silver. Is it good? Silver bar maybe

62454200
>>62450753
do you think the narrative actually influences sentiment after it's published?

62454605
>>62453044
Buy btc

62454692
>>62450950
Which fucking news?

62454719
It's over

62455393
>>62454200
What does it mean when we care the price alone

62455506
>>62450728
why not buy low cap gems
>Ansem
>Dogefi

62455701
>>62454719
or the opposite

62456393
>>62450728
bitcoin will converge with the performance of a 0.5x gold etf

62457047
>>62456393
Just that? I'll for memecoins

62457075
buy the dip

62457106
>>62450728
I need honest opinion. Shall I buy more?

62457313
I'm planning to throw 10k in BTC, is it a good time?

62458651
>>62457075
You can buy the dip or stake it. It's up to you, but I'd go with staking this time.

item.title: A P O LO G I Z E — 8 replies — 32.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
A P O LO G I Z E

Open thread • Replies: 8 • Updated: 2026-07-08 06:52 UTC

OP

right now

Early replies (first 3)

62455080
>>62455056
>stopped clock

62455105
>>62455056
>shit and piss down .3%
its so over

62455141
>>62455056
Sorry you didnt get to make your sequel movie 'The Small Long'

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62455272
>>62455056
>LULU

62455315
when do i dump msft for a quick buck...420 before earnings?

62455401
>>62455141
atleast I made a movie, where's yours?

62455433
>>62455401
Its called big white cock runs wild on your mom part 16

62458638
>>62455433
apologize chud

item.title: ITT: We pretend we’re zoomers — 15 replies — 7.5/hr
item.content:encoded:
ITT: We pretend we’re zoomers

Open thread • Replies: 15 • Updated: 2026-07-08 05:04 UTC

OP

I literally did everything society told me to do. I survived four grueling years at State U. I made a LinkedIn. I have a bachelor's. I even bought one interview shirt. And THIS is how I'm repaid? AI took half the jobs, offshoring took the rest, every HR roastie recruiter ghosts me because they can somehow tell I'm not one of their corporate NPCs, and boomers bought five-bedroom houses with a summer job. Don't tell me to relocate. Don't tell me to commute. Don't tell me to get roommates. Don't tell me to buy a starter home. Don't tell me to lower my expectations. I was promised a six-figure remote job, unlimited PTO, and my dream house by 24. If I don't have all of that, then clearly the system is broken...not me. I am the victim here.

Early replies (first 3)

62451138
My female peers are all sucking and fucking big millennial cock

62451340
I'm single and I need a 3 bed 2 bath house so I can play video games and masturbate in peace.

62451425
Hurr durr boomers all got free houses, free virgins and free ubereats avocado toast thats why im a fat smelly incel

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62451434
>>62451128
I don't get the obsession with zoomers.

62451447
>>62451128
I kinda miss when zoomers looked like this. It was very cute and endearing. Now zoomers look like haggard wraiths with how badly they're aging from all the stress, nicotine, and forever chemicals. Very sad generation, and I don't mean sad as in pathetic. Like genuinely sad. At least Millennials had a decent glimpse of the old world.

62451451
Hmm better get my hair curled into a big broccoli head

62451485
Things are so bad that I am rooting for this guy to kill some assholes and I am in my 40s. Lead head gens please get out of the way so we can fix things.

62451567
>>62451128
>>62451138
>>62451425
Holy seethe

62451617
>>62451128
which part of this is wrong though?

62451620
pic related is a 16 years old zoomer

u jelly?

62451690
>>62451620
Meddl.

62451726
>>62451128
I feel bad for later zoomers because they were sold on this lie that IT/tech was the next huge thing during the 2016-2022 boom (when a lot of zoomers were in college). Now the tech market is basically completely dead and shrinking even more ever day with big companies laying off workers and replacing them with AI or Indians (h1b or offshore).

62454736
>>62451128
boring ass thread lol

62454829
>>62451128
If you weren't part of the top 1% in 2026 before 30, you're part of the permanent underclass and your life is over

62458435
>>62451128
Lol you millenial uncs are washed and chopped. Youre obsessed with us and you dont got any rizz or aura

Redpill me on XDC.
Redpill me on HBAR.

62456743

shit, shit, feet.

62457523

here you go

62458546

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

item.title: Months from now… years from now… people will wish they had recognized what was sitting right in front of them for years. — 9 replies — 3.5/hr
item.content:encoded:
Months from now… years from now… people will wish they had recognized what was sitting right in front of them for years.

Open thread • Replies: 9 • Updated: 2026-07-08 04:30 UTC

OP

Months from now… years from now… people will wish they had recognized what was sitting right in front of them for years.

Most people think they’ll sell XRP and simply buy back lower.

They still don’t understand what happens when real utility creates real demand, real economic activity, and real liquidity. That’s a completely different market.

As more organizations connect to the network, demand doesn’t just appear… it compounds. That’s how multi-trillion dollar markets are built.

Humanity has never witnessed a utility driven financial transformation on this scale. For the first time, we get to live through it together.

Excited for the next chapter.

Early replies (first 3)

62450170
It's had more than a decade for all that, ain't happening champ

62450645
>>62450165
Xrp was $3.20 in 2018 lol, shut up faggot

62450808
>>62450170
>>62450645
Obtuse gay jewish fingers typed these.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62451330
So bullish

62453970
>>62450808

62453976
>>62450165
>Months from now
How can you not see the wisdom of owning a coin that is down over 50% in 6 months.. everyone that is selling is a fool... BUY more... PLEASE PLEASE PLEASE buy more... I have to sell before the rent is due!!!

62454861
So fucking bullish XRP

62456256
>>62450165
KABOOOOOOOOOOOOM
>>>62456234

62458355
>>62450165

item.title: Today is freedom day in burgerland, this means the CLARITY ACT has officially passed. — 32 replies — 115.1/hr
item.content:encoded:
Today is freedom day in burgerland, this means the CLARITY ACT has officially passed.

Open thread • Replies: 32 • Updated: 2026-07-08 06:47 UTC

OP

Today is freedom day in burgerland, this means the CLARITY ACT has officially passed.

Now many crypto's like XRP and Chainlink are no longer illegal and the banks can start buying it.

Are you ready for the pump of a lifetime in the coming weeks?

Early replies (first 3)

62444894
I just took 70mg of illicit amphetamine sulfate

62444895
>>62444894
That's very nice to hear, I hope you are enjoying yourself.

62444904
>>62444895
it's nothing like meth (oral ROA meth had no euphoria either), minimal vasoconstriction; none really. not euphoric like adderall either, it feels like super non-diuretic caffeine. this has a 1:1 dextro:racemic ratio; so the pharma equivalent is evekeo.

I thought it was bunk but my heart rate is elevated but no arrhythmia like meth (that shit is poison, it'll keep you up for 3-5 days with no perceptual fatigue at significant cost)

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62444906
>>62444895
I'm just irritated unless im working or reading something. which is the intended effect, it's just super mechanical caffeine idk how to describe it. it's impossible to "idle" on this shit, I have to be doing something.

62444911
>>62444904
>>62444906
okay well thats nice and all but not really what my thread was about

62444922
>>62444911
hm, do you know how it impacts privacy coins like XMR or even zioncash (ZEC)?

62444929
>>62444922
i do not

62444934
>>62444929
alright, I appreciate it regardless. thoughts on CBDCs?

62444939
>>62444934

I actually dont know much about that also

62444940
>>62444939
you sound like a nice guy no homo

62444948
these 2 got married y'know

62444959
>>62444940
Haha thanks man you too

>>62444948
Nooo we are just friends haha

62444978
>>62444892
> CLARITY ACT
wasn't there some announcement announced for yesterday?

62444991
op they haven't voted on it and aren't close to voting on it. they're hoping to get it done before august 10

62444995
>>62444991
Somone told me clarity act was 4th of july a few weeks ago

62445015
>>62444948
naw, the no homo made marriage impossible
>>62444959
>we are just friends
this

62445147
>>62444978
No, Trump did an interview restating that the US will be the crypto capital of the world.
>>62444892
CLARITY will pass, but there is a lot going behind the scenes that's causing it to stall. Did you know that CLARITY allows banks to buy and sell crypto? The last time US banks traded stocks was before the great depression.

62445160
>>62445147
Oh that's very interesting, thanks for sharing my friend

62445194
So, two more weeks?

62445212
>>62444948
you're responding to a samefag trying to keep his thread at the top because no one cares.

>>62444892
no one cares

62445217
>>62445212
that's not me

62445568
Why isnt crypto pumping harder?

62448360
>>62444892
The banks can already buy both. ETF inflows broke records despite being from small issuers on XRP, highest of any asset on the year, while inflows are borderline non-existent for link.

62448734
Everyone itt thread is a retarded bot.
Fake thread.

62450765
>>62444892
BOOOOOOOOOM

62450968
I hate niggers

62451473
>>62450968
Kaboom

62454381
>>62444892
>banks start buying it
okay jeet

62454867
>XRP

So. Fucking. Bullish.

62458344
>>62444892
KABOOOOOOOOOOOM

62458384
>>62444892
>this means the CLARITY ACT has officially passed.
nope

62458627
>>62444995
that date was always nonsense because they are literally in recess from the end of june until july 13. they will have another month to pass it before they go into recess yet again in august

item.title: /BBBY/ - Larry Cheng went onto Theppshow edition — 167 replies — 102.1/hr
item.content:encoded:
/BBBY/ - Larry Cheng went onto Theppshow edition

Open thread • Replies: 167 • Updated: 2026-07-08 05:26 UTC

OP

>rc buys 7,780,000 shares of BBBY, and $60-$80 strike calls worth 1,670,100 shares in March 2022

>rc sells shares and calls of BBBY in august 2022

>rc has a sitdown with a famous corporate raider, Carl Icahn in october 2022

>rc offers 400 million for BBBY in December 2022

>pitchbook (best M&A software suite in the world, costing 5 figures + per year) lists BBBY(now known as BBBYQ or DK-Butterfly-1) as sold via a leveraged buyout on January 13 2023

>a default on the ABL with JPM happened on the same day that pitchbook shows a leveraged buyout, the terms of that ABL specify that a default is triggered if a change of control happens, a change of control is defined as an acquisition of 40% or more of the voting power by any person or group.

>one month later, February 2023 RC tweets “someone tell the government to stop shooting down my balloons”, alluding to bad actors delaying the Bed Bath m&a

>the GameStop CEO then became a BBBYQ chapter 11 creditor and debtor

>Larry cheng (who helped RC build Chewy, and is a current boardmember at GameStop, and a close personal friend to RC) went onto theppshow podcast (which is dedicated to BBBY recovery theories) 8 months after BBBY filed for chapter 11, and 2 months after shares were cancelled

>OverStock - who is the most famous company to battle illegal naked shorts, who even released a crypto dividend 5 years ago - purchased the BBBY IP, and owns 55% of TZero - the only approved blockchain entity who can issue debt instruments and securities

>OverStock - whos now traded under ticker BBBY - has issued warrants with dates that match GME's warrants, and with a CUSIP-6 that matches BBBYQ's CUSIP-6, proving BBBYQ has already begun m&a activity with BBBY


REQUIRED READING: bbbwhy.com

>Larry Cheng and Bill Pulte have gone onto theppshow podcast

jake2b started a youtube channel to access quality BBBYQ easier than scrolling twitter posts:

https://www.youtube.com/@jake2b

previous thread:
>>62417410

Early replies (first 3)

62438274
>Larry Cheng was inside the PP show

62438733
Good morning my beautiful Towelkings.

62438734
uh... guys... you might want to check your accounts...

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62452287
>>62452270
Its right here can you stop whining like a fatherless attention starved faggot? Youre scaring the hoes.

https://x.com/Almaadeed/status/2072817361862717939

62452325
>>62452287
ok good it displays the gamestop.com preview on the twitter page but the link he posted is to teddy.com which redirects to gamestop.com

62452826
>>62450461
The silo around BBBYQ has become Papaliable

62454181
CUSIP-6

62454621
>>62454181
baste

62455277
Pulte, Ryan, and Sultan are cooking up Teddy literally as we speak. You can't make this shit up.

62455555
Mister Groobie?

62456168
>>62455277
Checked, GABBBYQ melting pot for these great men

62456559
>>62456487
Is this good?

62456600
>>62456559
It is alladeen

62456862
>>62456559
Let's just say that we won.

62456877
>>62437938
WAGMI

62457059
BATHROOM GODS
GET FUCKING READY
THIS IS THE ENDGAME
CONGRATULATIONS TO ALL THAT MADE IT THIS FAR
ENJOY!!!!!!!!!

62457184
Will this also benefit GME?
The other thread has too many shills to get an honest answer.

62457192
>>62457184
pools closed, this is a gated community and its also whites only.
head back

62457193
>>62457184
ultimately it's not going to benefit BBBY losers or GME owners.

it's meaningless noise signifying nothing except that the BBBY BK is winding ever closer to its finish without shareholders getting a dime.

62457200
>>62457193
that's because the bathroom gods will be getting way more than a dime

62457215
>>62457200
He asked for an honest answer and I gave it

you are getting nothing

if you get something from a class action lawsuit (unlikely) it's probably going to be less than a hundred dollars total. But in reality there's a long line of actual creditors and lawyers in front of you that will take any payments that might be won.

62457221
>>62457215
we won though

62457223
>>62457221
>we won though
retard of the year award perhaps.

62457418
>>62457215
less than a hundred per share aint bad tho.

I wonder what RC & Icahn are up too?

62457938
>>62449186
What is the legal process to uncancel a stock and reopen short positions that were closed upon cancelation? I can't seem to find it in current US securities law? AI can't find it for me either and is also unable to find me any cases of this ever happening. Looks to me like this is not possible under current law.

62457968
>>62457938
It's the exact same as if you were naked short a stock that issues a dividend. The company doesn't issue more money, they shorts simply are on the hook to pay for it.
Naked shorting creates synthetic longs. Disbursements to the shareholders by the estate/corporate entity are calculated by the actual TSO. It is not the prerogative of the company to "honor" counterfeits. The shorts pay for it.

62458028
>>62457938
what your looking for is the fact that the chapter 11 plan can still legally change, and equity/cash can be distributed to previous shareholders.

then what this poster said:
>>62457968

Short positions aren't reopened because they were never closed since the company is still in active chapter 11, and when the company give $ and shares to each previous shareholder they only have to make distributions based on the number of shares issued, not the extra billions that were created by market makers as Bed Bath pushed under 10 cents.

Nice try paid stock basher, but nothing you say can argue with reason.

62458266
>>62457938
There is no plan to “uncancel” the stock. That is a strawman argument you are forced to beat into the ground.

62458308
>>62457184
GAMEshire BATHaway
>t. pulte
>t. sultan

62458310
>>62458028
>Nice try paid stock basher
I think this whole thing must be some elaborate comedy bit, or you people are just insane. Good luck though.

62458385
>>62457184
>>62458308
It's as shrimple as that

62458481
>>62458308
>>62458385
Will this lead to MOASS?

item.title: Any active duty US military members or veterans on /biz/? — 144 replies — 50.2/hr
item.content:encoded:
Any active duty US military members or veterans on /biz/?

Open thread • Replies: 144 • Updated: 2026-07-08 04:12 UTC

OP

Any active duty US military members or veterans on /biz/?

I joined the military and I wanna know how to get a million dollars. I put 10% of my income into my retirement account and the rest of my free cash into index funds.

Early replies (first 3)

62447635
>>62447595
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=sZZaOR4UCnE

62447641
>>62447595
The only smart play is to aim for a 100% disability rating which you can get in ~1 to 4 years (1 enlistment).
I know several guys getting $4000+ a month for life.
I am a vet and I get nothing.

62447664
>>62447595
If you're not high rank or close to information in some other way then it's just another job. Not gonna change much in terms of how getting a million would look like, you'll have to gamble like the rest of us.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62456579
>>62456537
>>62456543
You live in a world that isn't reality, robert.
I've thoroughly debunked your claims ITT about the VA and false information about military finances.
Whenever you get caught LARPing you always fall back on the same schtick.
>Everyone who talks about the military is a recruiter
>If you say anything positive neutral or anything other than strictly negative about the military you are a recruiter
>Posting the same 3 images from 5 years ago you post on /k/ routinely as a "gotcha"
>switching IPs to ban evade
It's obvious each and every time you do it.
>>62456548
Your credibility is nil.

62456586
>>62456579
Those are two different guns. You would know this if you actually owned guns and didn't have to ask for permission from an armorer to access them. How clueless do you have to be to confuse a snub nosed revolver with an SAA?

62456598
>>62456579
>>62456586
the barrel lengths aren't anywhere close to being the same lmao

62456603
>>62456579
>>62456586
>>62456598
Also the sight, hammer, and grip are all different because they're completely different guns. I love when you get caught in an obvious lie.
>Your credibility is nil.
lel

62456606
>>62456586
>>62456598
>>62456603
Anyone can type the link in and see it's you posting all of that shit.

62456612
>>62456606
What link? The only link you should be opening is https://www.firearmsguide.com/ so you can learn to identify guns.

62456641
>>62456612
That's really besides the point though. The content of your posts in every thread you're in is the smoking gun.
It's your prerogative to derail any thread you're in.
If anyone dares to say any correct information regarding the military you take issue with it.
Each post is a confession from you robert. No one piece of evidence is the make-or-break for your laundry list of failed attempts to troll /meg/ or /pol/.

Just in this thread alone, I've foiled another attempt of yours and as a result I got you to admit to posting gay porn and post softcore gay porn ITT unprompted.
All because I dared to correct you about your lies about the VA.
Should I bring up your most recently failed LARP as the son of Graham Platner?

62456648
>>62456641
Except Robert is a dumb conservative racist chud, and I'm an anti-imperialist communist. Robert tried and failed to become an Epstein Warrior, whereas you succeeded in becoming one. I think you're both pathetic.

62456675
>>62456648
>Robert is a dumb conservative racist chud
>I'm an anti-imperialist communist
No, actually. Not too long ago you were rooting for "National Syndicalism" so you could argue with more people. You only started being a commie recently because no one else actually gives a shit about Syndicalism.
You are Robert, just some years of constant mindbrokenness and contrarianism apart.

62456736
>>62456675
I flirted with the idea of Fascism for a while, but even then I was looking at it from the left-fash tradition of guys like Edmondo Rossoni. Luckily, after doing more reading on theory and history, I realized that I was right when I was younger and Marxism is the only way forward. There's a reason the Epstein class has worked so tirelessly to discredit and defeat communism, and that's because it's the anti-Epstein ideology.

62456761
>>62456736
Yeah sure buddy, you're doing so much damage to the US by doordashing to single moms in Peoria, Illinois.

62456771
>>62456761
I don't have to do anything to damage America; it is collapsing in front of our eyes under the weight of its own contradictions. Zion Don is the purest essence of America, our spiritual identity distilled into a single man, and now the whole world sees what we really are beyond all the propaganda and lies. We are the Epstein Nation.

62456794
>>62456771
Ok you think pedos run the world, but how does this help OP who is a Jr. Officer in the military invest his money?
Do you see how you've basically hijacked this thread with SNCA?

It's the opposite of advice, you are a cognitohazard.

62456800
>>62456794
Everyone knows the ruling class that controls the economy and government are bunch of pedophiles. They're criminals, and they indulge in whatever crimes they can get away with. This is an obvious fact of life, and always has been.

62456828
>>62456800
Wrong answer. Let's try again!
What is the best financial move for OP who is a Jr. Officer in the military?

62456879
>>62456828
I have no idea because I've never been in the military and never wanted to.

62456897
>>62456891
Oooohhh that's two wrong in a row. Don't worry you still have one more shot at bringing the thread back on topic.

Help answer the topic of the thread by the gracious OP:
>I joined the military and I wanna know how to get a million dollars. I put 10% of my income into my retirement account and the rest of my free cash into index funds.

Grand prize is a free night stay at the Par-A-Dice Hotel and Casino! You wouldn't want to fuck this one up, Robert!

62456913
>>62456828
That question was answered forever ago before you faggots started your Robert script (note you were nowhere near the first ITT to call out the poster's retarded false claims about how VA works).

Keep investing in the TSP. The only thing I'd add is make sure he's putting it in the "C" Fund and not the retarded default "G" Fund.

Now will you fags stop hijacking OP's thread?

62456928
>>62456897
What's the topic?

62457039
>>62456528
Seeing as how they are gang stalking Robert for warning people about joining the military I would be interested in helping pay for a lawyer if I knew more about him. Maybe ask ACUL if they want to peruse this case (unlikely). Heard from other whistle blowers they all regularly say this sort of stuff when the camera is off. It is the ones who either say it on camera or on the internet that get punished. Considering that jannies and paid shills regularly break their own rules on /k/ I believe it.

https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=ABx4fG1ZJL4

VbMQveZu and Tgdq/+4p are glowie shills.

62457052
>>62457039
>gang stalking
Meds.

62457053
>>62457039
gang stalking isn't real. What I have are a couple of secret admirers who religiously read my twitter account and talk about me on 4chan all day.

62457076
>>62456736
Uh about that... Which brand of communism? East German with their discount Nazi uniforms? Figures since they were the most functional communists.

62457081
>>62456897
Tell him to wait for the bear market. 2030 sound good?

62457082
>>62457076
I'm a Maoist.

62457474
>>62447595
I'm a Veteran. 7 years. Officer Corps. I did not get rich but I saw a few of my peers get rich and this advice only works if you're an Officer (single Enlisted are stuck in the barracks. Sucks to suck).

1) Do not get married
2) Do not spend money on women
3) Choose an MOS that deploys FREQUENTLY (you are guaranteed to deploy more frequently than Special Forces if you go Air Defense in the Army).
3) Get a VA loan to buy a Quadplex at your first duty station.
4) VA loan can only be taken out to buy your primary residence but if you PCS, you now need a new primary residence. You've built 2-3 years of equity in the first property, which you now rent out through a property management company.

Bonus: Set up your own property management company and start managing other peoples properties, too.

5) Repeat Steps 3 and 4 until you get out of the military.

6) Go to the doctor for literally anything and everything. When you separate you will have so many service connected injuries that youll get 100% VA Disability rating ($4k tax free each month) and with that rating, free medical coverage through the VA (a massive savings in medical costs)

You can also abuse the infinite certification and education programs they offer (and you should). They say not to leave the military with less education than you had going in. Take advantage of that. If you ever find yourself in a bullshit job where you're not in the field every 3 months, and you're able to take every 4 day weekend off without hassle, use that time to build a business of some sort.

Anyone who joins the military with a plan of action in place can really turn their life around. Maybe not into millionaire status, but into a comfortable, never NEED to work again type of life.

62457579
>>62447595
the artstyle and color scheme on vanilla was so good its insane. we hit diminishing returns on polygons a long time ago

62458199
>>62457474
>2
Hookers are a military man's best friend. New Cars and divorces destroy so many finances.

Medical thing is critical, I had that taught to me and too many leave it on the table..

Deployment has to be something gay and retarded, nothing lethal

Business is hard to get into but basic money management with a budget sheet tracking your expenses are critical. The amount of shiny impulse buys add up and are easily forgotten about, hence a budget sheet

t. Military with a bs job

62458277
>>62456428
You could do the same with Manhattan too for $5,073 /mo. 36 months, not too bad. Get a hybrid course. I asked my coworker after posting this and he says he's down to going in person once a quarter

62458320
>>62457474
Wow what a fucking scam the military is

item.title: House buying — 91 replies — 39.5/hr
item.content:encoded:
House buying

Open thread • Replies: 91 • Updated: 2026-07-08 04:46 UTC

OP

I have $180k sitting in an S&P500 index fund and every normie I meet says I'm making a mistake by not getting a mortgage ASAP because housing will only get more expensive.

What is the /biz/ philosophy on this advice? I'm a rentoid and currently pay only around $1000 a month.

Early replies (first 3)

62447251
>>62447246
>every normie I meet says
You could buy and then the market severely crashes.
Housing shouldn't be a financial asset but that's corporatism for you.

62447257
>>62447251
Yea i guess but the stock market "could" also crash and i lose everything. That's not really insightful advice.

62447265
Lmao you are a rentoid

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62448682
>>62448659
>implying you're in the US
idiot. If we kick the foreigners out you'll be gone first.

62448689
Normies are not getting mortgages because rates are like 6.5% when they would not normally be 3%>.

62448696
>>62448689
Boomers paid 20%

62448701
>>62447286
>2nd house for 425k around 31.
>2nd house is worth 900k now
What happened is their dollars lost half the purchasing power.
BTW they didn't but the house for $425k. They bought it for that plus whatever they pay in interest over time. Which is very likely to be closer to the amount they sold it for later.
It's nice to build equity, but don't be a fool.

62449002
>>62447246
>I have $180k sitting in an S&P500 index fund and every normie I meet says I'm making a mistake by not getting a mortgage ASAP because housing will only get more expensive.
>What is the /biz/ philosophy on this advice? I'm a rentoid and currently pay only around $1000 a month.
I'm in a similar position to you and unless you have a lifestyle reason to need a house it doesn't make financial sense right now IMO. Look at it financially, houses cost probably on the ballpark of 300-350k, let's just assume 325k median in a low to medium cost area, that's a 145k loan, at 6.25% interest rate the annual loss of interest only of 9k on year 1 and 8.9k on year 2 contrasted with an annual net loss currently of 12k a year renting but you also dont have other costs like property taxes, insurance, and maintenance costs that should be baked into rent but are not going to baked into that interest loss of yhe house so add those in too, where i live I'd pay 6k prop tax, 1k insurance, and rule of thumb is to budget 1-3% of home value so 3.25k-9.8k. To build on the case if you have money in the SP500 it has returned 10.5% annually (18.9k) or if you want to play it safe 4.5% in CDs (8.1k). That said, it depends on how long you are looking to buy a house, how risk tolerant you are, and if your landlord wants to increase rents. Run those numbers down a spreadsheet and see what makes sense for you in your area but i have come to the conclusion of letting my money ride in the market for the time being knowing the risk that I could get burned.

62450291
>>62447246
my advice is quit snooping around your mom's secret book collection

62450516
average home price prior to covid was 4.5 years of salary; now its closer to 6.8 years of salary. prices are too high and is way cheaper to rent for now. i save while renting to buy outright, fuck a mortgage.

62450518
>>62448701
>nice to build equity
>depreciating asset requires upkeep
>no where to go but up

ive seen this movie before, sometime around 2008 i think

62450563
>>62450516
Thats a you problem if you cant double your wage post covid

62450591
I know this is biz but everyone here is talking about buying a house as though you have to make money off the decision, I could care less about making money off my house, I bought it because it was a huge lifestyle upgrade and now I live 100x better than when I was renting an apartment, it feels like I finally started my real life.

62450594
>>62447246
Let the boomers deal in the real world. Housing is a terrible investment if there is any risk to your income over the term of the loan. My cousin sold TSLA to put a down-payment on a home $120k. The most money he ever had. Home price was $230k. Turned out the home was a lemon and he had dumped another $80k into repairs. Guess what? He lost the house when he and his wife's winery side hustle failed. $200k given to bank for a couple years being a homeowner. Had he put the money into an index fund he'd be worth half a million by now.

62450595
houses are for living in, not speculation

62450600
>>62450594
the math on this story isn't adding up, bro had a $110K loan after that down payment which is only like $500 a month, sounds like he was just bad at finance if he lost it with that payment

62450606
>>62448058
Jealous blk boi

62450608
>>62450600
Rentoids all suck at math their anecdotes are fake as fuck but real in their bitter minds

62450897
My house is paid off. Feels great man. 6 bedrooms 3.5 baths on half an acre. Great home for my family of 6.

62451180
>>62450600
You can't even follow basic math. He had a $190k loan at around 6%. Hardly $500 a month with insurance, taxes, mortgage insurance, etc.

>>62450608
My cost to rent is 11% of my take home pay. You are spending more on a mortgage with no guarantee of keeping your home.

Biz really has the dumbest anons.

62455019
>>62447246
When you buy a house, if you do, if you're going to rent it out, make sure you put a large enough down payment to make it cash flow $600+/mo.
Basically getting your money back little by little
and that 600 surplus will cover expenses as they come up, and someone else pays the equity for you, and you get the interest & depreciation deductions.
One big mistake with my rentals is putting the minimum down.
Expenses come out of my pocket mostly.
Remember taxes go up, insurance goes up, rezoned to a FEMA flood zone to lay for coastal homes, HOA goes up, everything goes up, so even if you increase rent, you're not taking it home, because you're paying those increased costs which go up every year.
You can make money in RE if you can fix the places yourself. If you're not handy? Think about something else.
>t. not handy and it costs me an arm and a leg and construction contractors are the worst, even the "premium" ones can't install a level floor.

I gave up on getting a house. I want to live in a cheap RV but even RV park rent here is over $1 a month.

62458370
>>62458331
I looked into this clear back in the 1990's. I figured just buy some cheap land and park a mobile home on it. I live in a rural area that used to be super libertarian anti-government so I thought I could do it.

it wasn't legal even back then. I had to park in a trailer park and pay lot rent which was just as much as renting an apt. The rules are stupid, they don't want people living in an affordable manner. They want you buying houses and propping up the prices.
